---
title: "Negative Binomial Modeling"
author: "Ian Sargent"
date: '`r Sys.Date()`'
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

### Packages
```{r}
# Load packages with pacman library
pacman::p_load(
  tidyverse, # Data manipulation
  sf, # Working with simple feature geometries
  janitor, # Column name cleaning
  MASS, # For negative binomial regression model
  fixest, # Fixed effects model
  randomForest, # For random forest feature selection
  vip, # Variable importance plot for random forest
  glmnet, # LASSO regression / regularization (also feature selection)
  mpath
)
```

### Read Data
```{r}
nb_data <- read_csv("https://raw.githubusercontent.com/iansargent/DC-Metro-Crime/refs/heads/main/Data/Combined/nb_data.csv", show_col_types = FALSE) |>
  dplyr::select(-c(line))
```


## Predicting Violent Crimes

### Feature Selection Methods

#### Determining Best Distance Metric by AIC
```{r}
# Including all distance variables included
nb_violent <- fixest::fenegbin(
  violent_crimes ~ stations_within_800m + stations_within_400m + dist_to_nearest_metro +
    above_ground + transfer_station +
    green_yellow_line + blue_orange_silver_line +
    median_household_income + pct_poverty + pct_unemployed +
    pct_black_nh + pct_hispanic + pct_white + pct_renter + pct_vacant +
    pct_bachelors_only + pct_graduate + offset(log_pop) |
    factor(year) + factor(month),
  cluster = ~tract,
  data = nb_data
)
```


#### Random Forest Feature Selection
```{r}
# Set a random seed
set.seed(123)

# Select model variables and drop incomplete rows
rf_data_v <- nb_data |>
  dplyr::select(
    violent_crimes, dist_to_nearest_metro, above_ground, transfer_station,
    stations_within_400m, stations_within_800m,
    red_line, green_yellow_line, blue_orange_silver_line,
    median_household_income, pct_poverty, pct_unemployed,
    pct_black_nh, pct_hispanic, pct_renter, pct_vacant,
    pct_bachelors_only
  )

# Run the random forest predicting violent crimes (with 500 trees)
rf_violent <- randomForest(
  violent_crimes ~ .,
  data = rf_data_v,
  ntree = 100,
  importance = TRUE,
  na.action = na.roughfix # Impute NA values
)

# Create a variable importance barplot showing which variables were the most selected to predict violent crime counts
importance(rf_violent) |>
  as.data.frame() |>
  rownames_to_column("variable") |>
  arrange(desc(`%IncMSE`)) |>
  mutate(variable = fct_reorder(variable, `%IncMSE`)) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = `%IncMSE`, y = variable)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#2980b9") +
  labs(
    title    = "Random Forest Feature Importance — Violent Crime",
    subtitle = "% Increase in MSE when variable is permuted",
    x        = "% Increase in MSE",
    y        = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)
```


#### LASSO Regression (Regularization)
```{r}
# Random Seed
set.seed(123)

lasso_df_v <- nb_data |>
  dplyr::select(
    -c(
      tract, year, month, nearest_station_id, property_crimes, total_crimes
    )
  ) |>
  drop_na()

# Build components
X_v <- lasso_df_v[, -1] |>
  as.matrix() |>
  scale()

y_v <- lasso_df_v$violent_crimes

offset_v <- lasso_df_v$log_pop

theta_hat_v <- nb_violent$theta


# Violent crime LASSO with cross-validation (NB family)
cv_lasso_v <- cv.glmreg(
  x = X_v,
  y = y_v,
  family = "negbin",
  theta = theta_hat_v,
  alpha = 1,
  offset = offset_v,
  nfolds = 10
)

surviving_v <- coef(cv_lasso_v,  s = cv_lasso_v$lambda.optim) |>
  as.matrix() |>
  as.data.frame() |>
  dplyr::arrange(order_by = -abs(V1))

surviving_v |>
  knitr::kable(digits = 4, caption = "Regularized Variables — Violent Crime")

cv_lasso_v$lambda.optim
ncol(X_v)
sum(coef(cv_lasso_v,  s = cv_lasso_v$lambda.optim) != 0) - 1
theta_hat_v
```


## Predicting Property Crimes

### Feature Selection

#### Best Distance Metric by AIC
```{r}
# Including all distance variables
nb_property <- fixest::fenegbin(
  property_crimes ~ stations_within_800m + stations_within_400m + dist_to_nearest_metro +
    above_ground + transfer_station +
    green_yellow_line + blue_orange_silver_line +
    median_household_income + pct_poverty + pct_unemployed +
    pct_black_nh + pct_hispanic + pct_white + pct_renter + pct_vacant +
    pct_bachelors_only + pct_graduate + offset(log_pop) |
    factor(year) + factor(month),
  cluster = ~tract,
  data = nb_data
)
```


#### Random Forest Feature Importance
```{r}
rf_data_p <- nb_data |>
  dplyr::select(
    property_crimes, dist_to_nearest_metro, above_ground, transfer_station,
    red_line, green_yellow_line, blue_orange_silver_line,
    median_household_income, pct_poverty, pct_unemployed,
    pct_black_nh, pct_hispanic, pct_renter, pct_vacant,
    pct_bachelors_only
  )


rf_property <- randomForest(
  property_crimes ~ .,
  data = rf_data_p,
  ntree = 100,
  importance = TRUE,
  na.action = na.roughfix
)

importance(rf_property) |>
  as.data.frame() |>
  rownames_to_column("variable") |>
  arrange(desc(`%IncMSE`)) |>
  mutate(variable = fct_reorder(variable, `%IncMSE`)) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = `%IncMSE`, y = variable)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#2980b9") +
  labs(
    title    = "Random Forest Feature Importance — Property Crime",
    subtitle = "% Increase in MSE when variable is permuted",
    x        = "% Increase in MSE",
    y        = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)
```


#### LASSO Regression

```{r}
# Random Seed
set.seed(123)

lasso_df_p <- nb_data |>
  dplyr::select(-c(tract, year, month, nearest_station_id, violent_crimes, total_crimes)) |>
  drop_na()

# Build components
X_p <- lasso_df_p[, -1] |>
  as.matrix() |>
  scale()

y_p <- lasso_df_p$property_crimes

offset_p <- lasso_df_p$log_pop

theta_hat_p <- nb_property$theta


# Violent crime LASSO with cross-validation (NB family)
cv_lasso_p <- cv.glmreg(
  x = X_p,
  y = y_p,
  family = "negbin",
  theta = theta_hat_p,
  alpha = 1,
  offset = offset_p,
  nfolds = 10
)

surviving_p <- coef(cv_lasso_p, s = cv_lasso_p$lambda.optim) |>
  as.matrix() |>
  as.data.frame() |>
  dplyr::arrange(order_by = -abs(V1))

surviving_p |>
  knitr::kable(digits = 4, caption = "Regularized Variables — Violent Crime")

cv_lasso_p$lambda.optim
ncol(X_p)
sum(coef(cv_lasso_p,  s = cv_lasso_v$lambda.optim) != 0) - 1
theta_hat_p
```


```{r}
cv_lasso_v$lambda.optim  # violent
cv_lasso_p$lambda.optim  # property

sum(coef(cv_lasso_v, s = cv_lasso_v$lambda.optim) != 0) - 1
sum(coef(cv_lasso_p, s = cv_lasso_p$lambda.optim) != 0) - 1
```

## Model Evaluation

### Rootograms
```{r}
rootogram_data <- function(model, outcome, max_count = 30) {
  # Observed frequencies
  obs <- table(factor(outcome, levels = 0:max_count))
  counts <- as.numeric(names(obs))

  # Predicted frequencies using NB distribution
  mu <- fitted(model)
  theta <- model$theta

  expected <- sapply(counts, function(k) {
    sum(dnbinom(k, size = theta, mu = mu))
  })

  tibble(
    count    = counts,
    observed = as.numeric(obs),
    expected = expected,
    sqrt_obs = sqrt(as.numeric(obs)),
    sqrt_exp = sqrt(expected),
    diff     = sqrt_obs - sqrt_exp # hanging bar height
  )
}

# Build rootogram data
root_v <- rootogram_data(nb_violent, nb_data$violent_crimes)
root_p <- rootogram_data(nb_property, nb_data$property_crimes)

# Plot function
plot_rootogram <- function(root_df, title) {
  ggplot(root_df, aes(x = count)) +
    # Hanging bars — drop from sqrt(expected) down by diff
    geom_rect(aes(
      xmin = count - 0.4,
      xmax = count + 0.4,
      ymin = sqrt_exp - sqrt_obs,
      ymax = sqrt_exp
    ), fill = "steelblue", color = "white") +
    # Expected curve
    geom_line(aes(y = sqrt_exp), color = "red", linewidth = 0.8) +
    geom_point(aes(y = sqrt_exp), color = "red", size = 1.5) +
    # Zero reference line
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "solid", color = "black") +
    labs(
      title    = title,
      x        = "Crime Count",
      y        = "√ Frequency"
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12)
}

plot_rootogram(root_v, "Violent Crimes: Fitted vs Actual Counts")
plot_rootogram(root_p, "Property Crimes: Fitted vs Actual Counts")
```
